steps.title: Focus on the conclusion
steps.content: Romero and colleagues concluded that snowball sampling may be superior to purposive sampling for recruiting rural residents with the desired characteristics. A supporting finding must compare those two methods on that specific recruitment outcome.

steps.title: Reject off-target findings
steps.content: A says recruits differed from initial participants, not that recruitment improved. B concerns prior study participation. D compares snowball sampling with untargeted contacting rather than purposive sampling. A direct finding that snowball sampling produced more qualifying participants supports the conclusion. The answer is C.
